machinery_genes.quote: The proteins EscD and EscJ form two concentric rings in the inner membrane (IM) and the EscC protein forms the outer membrane (OM) ring
machinery_genes.note: EPEC LEE-encoded injectisome (Esc/Sep). EscC = SctC/secretin family ('EscC belongs to the secretin family of proteins that function as channels for secretion of bacterial proteins across the OM').

machinery_genes.quote: The proteins EscD and EscJ form two concentric rings in the inner membrane (IM) and the EscC protein forms the outer membrane (OM) ring
machinery_genes.note: EscD = SctD-family IM ring; 'EscD is believed to form the outermost IM ring that surrounds the EscJ ring' (PrgH/YscD/MxiG homolog).

machinery_genes.quote: Multiple protein alignment of the C-terminal domain of the lipoproteins EscJ (EPEC), MxiJ (), PrgK (), and YscJ () forming the inner membrane ring
machinery_genes.note: EscJ = SctJ lipoprotein; anchored to the outer leaflet of the IM, forms periplasmic ring on top of the IM.

machinery_genes.quote: in A/E pathogens, is built of five different proteins: EscR, EscS, EscT, EscU, and EscV (EscRSTUV)
machinery_genes.note: EscR = SctR (SpaP/YscR homolog); one of the five membrane-embedded export-apparatus proteins.

machinery_genes.quote: in A/E pathogens, is built of five different proteins: EscR, EscS, EscT, EscU, and EscV (EscRSTUV)
machinery_genes.note: EscS = SctS (SpaQ/YscS homolog); export-apparatus component.

machinery_genes.quote: in A/E pathogens, is built of five different proteins: EscR, EscS, EscT, EscU, and EscV (EscRSTUV)
machinery_genes.note: EscT = SctT (SpaR/YscT homolog); export-apparatus component.

machinery_genes.quote: EscU belongs to the YscU/FlhB family of proteins... a cytoplasmic C-terminal domain of ca. 100 amino acids that undergoes autocleavage in its NPTH amino acid motif
machinery_genes.note: EscU = SctU (SpaS/YscU homolog); autocleaving export-gate switch protein.

machinery_genes.quote: EscV, the major constituent of the T3 export apparatus, is also composed of two main domains: an N-terminal region with eight transmembrane helices and a C-terminal cytoplasmic domain
machinery_genes.note: EscV = SctV (InvA/MxiA/HrcV homolog); FlhA/YscV family nonameric export-gate ring.

machinery_genes.quote: Here we characterize EscN, an enteropathogenic Escherichia coli type III ATPase... Our results demonstrate that EscN is a Mg2+-dependent ATPase
machinery_genes.note: EscN = SctN (InvC/YscN/Spa47/HrcN homolog). Andrade et al 2007 biochemically characterize the EPEC EscN ATPase; Gaytan 2016 also names it ('The ATPase complex is formed by the EscN, EscL, and EscO proteins').

machinery_genes.quote: The ATPase complex is formed by the EscN, EscL, and EscO proteins... EscN interacts with EscL, a negative regulator that inhibits its ATPase activity
machinery_genes.note: EscL = SctL (OrgB/YscL/MxiN homolog), FliH-like; also implicated in the sorting platform.

machinery_genes.quote: The ATPase complex is formed by the EscN, EscL, and EscO proteins... and with EscO, which conversely, stimulates EscN ATPase activity
machinery_genes.note: EscO = SctO (InvI/YscO/Spa13 homolog, FliJ-related); formerly EscA. Stimulates EscN oligomerization/ATPase activity.

machinery_genes.quote: the C-ring is proposed to be a cytoplasmic annular structure located at the base of the basal body, formed mainly by the EscQ protein (formerly known as SepQ), which belongs to the YscQ/FliN (SctQ) protein family
machinery_genes.note: EscQ = SctQ (SpaO/YscQ/Spa33 homolog); formerly SepQ. C-ring / sorting-platform core.

machinery_genes.quote: the sorting platform might be formed by EscQ, EscL, and EscK (formerly Orf4), a component that has been associated with the SctK protein family
machinery_genes.note: EscK = SctK (OrgA/MxiK homolog); formerly Orf4. 'EscK is critical for T3 secretion'.

machinery_genes.quote: the OM and IM rings are connected through a periplasmic inner rod formed by the oligomerization of the EscI protein
machinery_genes.note: EscI = SctI (PrgJ/YscI/MxiI homolog); inner-rod subunit.

machinery_genes.quote: The needle is a superhelical hollow structure comprised of multiple copies of the EscF protein (Wilson et al.,), which is essential for the secretion of all T3 substrates
machinery_genes.note: EscF = SctF (PrgI/YscF/MxiH homolog); needle filament subunit.

machinery_genes.quote: EscP, which directly interacts with EscF, measures needle length upon intermittent secretion during needle assembly
machinery_genes.note: EscP = SctP (InvJ/YscP/Spa32 homolog); T3S4 molecular-ruler protein.

machinery_genes.role: extracellular filament forming the secretion conduit
machinery_genes.quote: it is assembled by the polymerization of multiple subunits of the EspA protein... forming a hollow structure of 12 nm in width that allows the passage of substrates through a 2.5 nm central channel
machinery_genes.note: EspA = EPEC/EHEC-specific filament extending the needle; hydrophilic translocator. Apparatus per brief (forms the secretion conduit), not an effector.

machinery_genes.quote: The translocation pore is a protein complex formed by the hetero-oligomerization of EspB and EspD subunits
machinery_genes.note: EspD = hydrophobic translocator (YopB/IpaB/SipB analog); dominant pore-former. Apparatus per brief (host-membrane pore).

machinery_genes.quote: The translocation pore is a protein complex formed by the hetero-oligomerization of EspB and EspD subunits
machinery_genes.note: EspB = hydrophobic translocator (YopD/IpaC/SipC analog). Counted as apparatus per brief; note it is also translocated into host cells at low levels, but its structural role is pore formation.

machinery_genes.quote: It comprises proteins like SepL from A/E pathogens... MxiC from(), InvE and SsaL fromSPI-1 and SPI-2, respectively... YopN/TyeA from()
machinery_genes.note: SepL = SctW gatekeeper (MxiC/InvE/YopN-TyeA homolog); 'SepL also interacts with the export gate component EscV'. Apparatus-associated gatekeeper, not a transcriptional regulator.

machinery_genes.quote: Molecular switch 2: SepL/SepD  The second molecular switch regulates differentially the secretion of translocators and effectors
machinery_genes.note: SepD = SepL partner; deletion phenocopies sepL. Lower-confidence machinery call: it is a secretion-regulatory apparatus component (gatekeeper module), not a transcriptional regulator. No standalone Sct designation; SsaM/Pcr2/SycN-class partner.

machinery_genes.role: peptidoglycan-lytic enzyme required for injectisome assembly
machinery_genes.quote: In EPEC this task is accomplished by the use of a specialized PG degrading enzyme named EtgA (Pallen et al.,) that localizes to the periplasm to exert its PG lytic activity and which is required for efficient T3SS assembly
machinery_genes.note: EtgA = dedicated T3SS lytic transglycosylase (IagB/IpgF-class). Interacts with inner rod EscI; required for assembly, hence apparatus. Lower-confidence-of-inclusion call (specialized accessory, not a core ring/export-gate gene).

notes: System = the EPEC LEE-encoded injectisome (Esc/Sep nomenclature), the archetypal attaching-and-effacing T3SS. Although this instance's sys_instance_label is 'prophage_PP4' and the curated effectors (E2348C_3231-3233) are non-LEE effectors, the T3SS APPARATUS itself is encoded on the LEE pathogenicity island (inserted at selC), not on prophage PP4; PP4 and other prophages carry secreted effectors that exploit the LEE machinery. Per the separated-paper rule I followed the founding LEE-sequence work (Elliott et al 1998, Mol Microbiol, which sequenced the complete LEE of this exact strain E2348/69; Perna et al 1998 catalogued the 41 shared LEE genes) and used the comprehensive structural review of Gaytán et al 2016 (Front Cell Infect Microbiol), which names every Esc/Sep component verbatim, as the recognition source for naming quotes. EscN's ATPase identity is additionally anchored to Andrade et al 2007, an EPEC-specific biochemical characterization. Core apparatus fully covered gene-by-gene: OM secretin (EscC), IM rings (EscD, EscJ), five-protein export apparatus (EscRSTUV), ATPase complex (EscN/EscL/EscO), C-ring/sorting platform (EscQ/EscK), inner rod (EscI), needle (EscF), molecular ruler (EscP), the EPEC-specific filament + translocation-pore translocon (EspA/EspB/EspD), the gatekeeper module (SepL/SepD), and the assembly-dedicated PG lytic enzyme EtgA. Lowest-confidence calls: SepD (gatekeeper partner, regulatory-apparatus rather than a structural ring) and EtgA (specialized accessory lytic transglycosylase, not a core ring/export gene) - both retained because they are documented secretion-apparatus components, not transcriptional regulators. DELIBERATELY EXCLUDED as non-machinery per the brief: secreted effectors (Tir, Map, EspF, EspG, EspH, EspZ, NleA, and the PP4 effectors E2348C_3231-3233); transcriptional regulators (Ler, GrlA, GrlR); chaperones (CesAB, CesD, CesD2, CesT, CesF, CesL, EscE, EscG); and intimin (Eae), which is an OM adhesin / host-receptor protein for intimate attachment, not part of the secretion conduit. All quotes are verbatim from the cited PMC full text (Gaytán 2016) or the cited abstract (Andrade 2007). Citation note (PubMed): see https://doi.org/10.3389/fcimb.2016.00129 , https://doi.org/10.1016/j.abb.2007.09.020 , https://doi.org/10.1046/j.1365-2958.1998.00783.x , https://doi.org/10.1128/IAI.66.8.3810-3817.1998 .
